evals.name: vertical-slice-projects-feature
evals.prompt: In this Katalyst project (samples/katalyst-example uses package io.github.darkryh.katalyst.example), add a complete 'projects' feature: an entity Project with fields id, name, ownerEmail, and createdAtMillis; its table; a repository; a service with create(name, ownerEmail) and listAll(); and REST routes POST /api/projects and GET /api/projects. Follow the project's existing conventions so it is auto-discovered and compiles.
evals.expected_output: Entity implements Identifiable<Long> with override val id: Long? = null; table is a LongIdTable implementing Table<Long, Project> with a mapping{} block (generatedId/field/construct); repository implements CrudRepository<Long, Project> with override val table; service implements Service and wraps writes in transactionManager.transaction{}; routes use fun Route.xxx()=katalystRouting{} with call.ktInject<ProjectService>(); all imports use io.github.darkryh.katalyst.* and Exposed org.jetbrains.exposed.v1.*; NO annotation-based DI.

evals.name: bootstrap-postgres-events-scheduler
evals.prompt: Write the main() bootstrap for a new Katalyst service that uses a PostgreSQL database loaded from YAML, runs on Netty, and enables events and the scheduler. Also show the application.yaml database section. Make it boot correctly.
evals.expected_output: katalystApplication(args){} with engine(NettyServer), beanEngine(KoinBeanEngine), enableYamlConfiguration(), database{ fromConfiguration() }, scanPackages(...), schema{...}, features{ enableEvents(); enableScheduler() }; correct import paths (di.katalystApplication, koin.KoinBeanEngine, ktor.engine.netty.NettyServer, config.yaml.enableYamlConfiguration, di.feature.enableEvents, scheduler.enableScheduler); yaml has database.url/username/password/driver. Recognizes engine + beanEngine + database + scanPackages are required.

evals.name: transaction-aware-event
evals.prompt: In a Katalyst service, I want to publish a ProjectCreatedEvent when a project is created, and have a handler that sends a notification — but the notification must NOT happen if the database transaction rolls back. Show the event, the service publishing it, and the handler.
evals.expected_output: Event implements DomainEvent (plain data class); service injects EventBus and calls eventBus.publish(...) INSIDE transactionManager.transaction{} so delivery defers to commit; handler implements EventHandler<ProjectCreatedEvent> with override val eventType and suspend fun handle; explains transaction-aware/deferred-until-commit behavior; handler discovered by implementing the interface (no annotation).

evals.name: dep-setup-web-persistence
evals.prompt: I'm starting a brand-new Katalyst service from scratch. Write the dependencies { } block of my build.gradle.kts for a web service backed by a database — I need HTTP routes and persistence (an embedded H2 is fine for now). Use the current recommended way to depend on Katalyst, and tell me which versions I have to specify.
evals.expected_output: Uses platform("io.github.darkryh.katalyst:katalyst-bom:1.0.0-alpha01") for version alignment; adds katalyst-starter-web and katalyst-starter-persistence with NO version each; explains that only the BOM carries a version and starters bring Ktor/Netty/Exposed/HikariCP/H2 transitively. Does NOT list individual modules (katalyst-core/-di/-ktor/-persistence/-ktor-engine-netty) and does NOT add Ktor/Exposed/HikariCP/H2 as explicit dependencies.

assertions.text: Starter coordinates carry NO explicit version (only the BOM does)
Does NOT declare individual modules like katalyst-core, katalyst-di, katalyst-ktor, or katalyst-ktor-engine-netty
Does NOT add Ktor, Exposed, HikariCP, or H2 as explicit third-party dependencies

evals.name: dep-add-features-and-test
evals.prompt: My Katalyst app's build.gradle.kts already declares the Katalyst BOM and katalyst-starter-web. I now need to add WebSockets, the scheduler, and the ability to write integration tests. Which dependency lines do I add?
evals.expected_output: Adds katalyst-starter-websockets and katalyst-starter-scheduler (implementation) and katalyst-starter-test (testImplementation); all WITHOUT explicit versions because the BOM is already present; does NOT tell the user to add ktor-server-websockets, the bare katalyst-scheduler/katalyst-websockets modules, or katalyst-testing-core/-ktor directly.

assertions.text: New starter lines carry NO explicit version (BOM already present)
Does NOT instruct adding individual modules (ktor-server-websockets, katalyst-scheduler, katalyst-testing-core/-ktor)
